CREATE TABLE employees (id INT PRIMARY KEY, emp_name VARCHAR(100), dep_name VARCHAR(100), salary DECIMAL(10, 2));
CREATE TABLE departments (id INT PRIMARY KEY, dep_name VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO departments (id, dep_name) VALUES (1, 'HR'), (2, 'Finance'), (3, 'Engineering');
INSERT INTO employees (id, emp_name, dep_name, salary) VALUES (1, 'Alice', 'HR', 50000), (2, 'Bob', 'Finance', 70000), (3, 'Charlie', 'Engineering', 80000);
SELECT emp_name, salary FROM employees WHERE salary > 60000 ORDER BY salary DESC;
SELECT dep_name, AVG(salary) AS avg_salary FROM employees GROUP BY dep_name HAVING AVG(salary) > 60000;
SELECT e.emp_name, d.dep_name FROM employees e INNER JOIN departments d ON e.dep_name = d.dep_name;
SELECT emp_name FROM employees WHERE emp_name LIKE 'A%';
SELECT id, emp_name, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY salary) AS rank FROM employees;
SELECT dep_name, COUNT(*) AS emp_count FROM employees GROUP BY dep_name;
SELECT id, emp_name FROM employees WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM employees WHERE salary > 60000);
SELECT dep_name, SUM(salary) AS total_salary FROM employees GROUP BY dep_name UNION SELECT 'Total', SUM(salary) FROM employees;
SELECT e.emp_name, LEAD(e.emp_name) OVER (ORDER BY salary) AS next_emp FROM employees e;
SELECT e.emp_name, NTH_VALUE(e.emp_name, 2) OVER (PARTITION BY e.dep_name ORDER BY e.salary) AS second_highest FROM employees e;
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employees) AS total_employees, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM departments) AS total_departments;
SELECT * FROM (VALUES (1, 'cero'), (2, 'uno'), (3, 'dos')) AS number_words(num, word) WHERE num < 3;
SELECT e.emp_name, e.salary, d.dep_name FROM employees e LEFT JOIN departments d ON e.dep_name = d.dep_name WHERE d.id IS NULL;
SELECT CURRENT_DATE, (1 + 1) * 2, pi();
SELECT e.emp_name, SUM(e.salary) OVER (PARTITION BY e.dep_name ORDER BY e.salary ROWS BETWEEN 1 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS running_total FROM employees e ORDER BY e.dep_name, e.salary;
SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY d.dep_name) AS dep_rank, d.dep_name FROM departments d;
SELECT e.dep_name, e.emp_name FROM employees e WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM departments d WHERE d.dep_name = e.dep_name);
